title: One UI can orchestrate multiple isolated tools
canonical_summary: A Gradio front end can act as a control surface while separate backend scripts run in their own virtual environments.
source_priority_rule: Architecture notes and actual process-launch design outrank generic UI examples.
answer_pattern: Explain front-end orchestration vs backend dependency isolation.
exactness_guard: Do not imply one Python process can safely import conflicting stacks.

title: Subprocess boundaries protect dependency stacks
canonical_summary: Launching tools as subprocesses from their own venvs can prevent dependency conflicts from crashing the main UI environment.
source_priority_rule: Process boundary design outranks convenience imports.
answer_pattern: Explain subprocess isolation and why imports are riskier.
exactness_guard: Do not recommend importing every module into the Gradio app.

title: The UI should pass files and parameters, not hidden state
user_phrasing: how should gradio communicate with tools
canonical_summary: Multi-venv tools should communicate through explicit files, parameters, JSON, stdout/stderr, or defined APIs rather than hidden shared state.
source_priority_rule: Explicit interface design outranks ad-hoc coupling.
answer_pattern: Recommend explicit interfaces and log capture.
exactness_guard: Do not create invisible coupling that cannot be debugged.

title: Each module needs its own health check
canonical_summary: Each isolated backend should have a lightweight health check so the UI can report whether that module is ready.
source_priority_rule: Health-check design should be source-backed by the module contract.
answer_pattern: Explain health checks as a way to avoid blind failures.
exactness_guard: Do not assume subprocess launch means the tool is usable.

title: Module works alone but fails from UI
user_phrasing: script works alone but not gradio
backend fails when launched from app
canonical_summary: This often means the UI is launching the wrong interpreter, wrong working directory, or wrong environment variables.
source_priority_rule: Actual subprocess command and captured logs drive diagnosis.
answer_pattern: Check launch command, working directory, env activation, and logs.
exactness_guard: Do not rewrite the model workflow before checking the process boundary.

title: Dependency conflict inside single UI env
canonical_summary: Trying to load conflicting AI tool stacks into one venv can cause dependency conflicts; isolate backends when conflicts appear.
source_priority_rule: Dependency logs and environment design guide the fix.
answer_pattern: Recommend isolating conflicting tools behind subprocess/module boundaries.
exactness_guard: Do not keep piling packages into one venv after conflicts appear.

title: UI freezes during long backend job
canonical_summary: A long backend job can block the UI if queueing, subprocess handling, or progress polling is not designed correctly.
source_priority_rule: UI event model and process design drive diagnosis.
answer_pattern: Recommend queue/progress handling and non-blocking subprocess design.
exactness_guard: Do not diagnose UI freeze as only a model problem.

title: Import module vs subprocess module
canonical_summary: If dependencies are compatible and lightweight, import may be okay. If the tool has heavy/conflicting dependencies, use subprocess or service boundary.
answer_pattern: Choose the simplest boundary that preserves stability.
exactness_guard: Do not default to imports for heavy AI stacks.

title: Single app issue vs module ecosystem design
user_phrasing: build one gradio app or plugin system
canonical_summary: If only one tool is being wrapped, keep the app simple. If many independent tools will be added, design a module registry and per-module config.
answer_pattern: Separate one-off wrapper from modular ecosystem.
exactness_guard: Do not overbuild a plugin system for one script.

title: Trust captured subprocess logs
user_phrasing: what source matters for gradio backend failure
canonical_summary: For multi-venv orchestration, captured stdout/stderr, exit codes, environment paths, and command lines outrank UI assumptions.
answer_pattern: Ask for command/log evidence before changing architecture.
exactness_guard: Do not debug from UI symptoms alone.

title: Do not confuse orchestration with model serving
canonical_summary: Gradio orchestration coordinates tools and UI. Model serving exposes a model through an API/runtime. They can overlap but are not identical.
answer_pattern: Explain the difference between tool orchestration and model serving.
exactness_guard: Do not label every Gradio backend as a model server.

title: How to answer Gradio multi-venv questions
user_phrasing: how should assistant answer multi venv gradio
canonical_summary: Answers should identify the module boundary, recommend a communication method, capture logs, and keep dependency stacks isolated.
answer_pattern: Use: UI role -> backend boundary -> IO contract -> logs -> health check.
exactness_guard: Do not hide process boundaries from the user.
